input.title: Restaurant Management System
input.description: Restaurant Management System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

scope: Menu Item Variants and Modifiers workflows
Digital Menu QR Code Generation workflows
constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Menu Management with Categories
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end menu management with categories capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Menu Management with Categories should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to menu management with categories without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within menu management with categories and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-02.title: Menu Item Variants and Modifiers
requirements: The system must support menu item variants and modifiers as a complete business workflow within the application.
Menu Item Variants and Modifiers should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with menu item variants and modifiers from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to menu item variants and modifiers for operational support.

FR-01.title: Plan Menu Management with Categories Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for menu management with categories and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: The system must provide end-to-end menu management with categories capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records; Menu Management with Categories should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate; Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to menu management with categories without leaving the module context.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for menu management with categories is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for menu management with categories are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for menu management with categories can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Menu Management with Categories Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for menu management with categories on React, Node.js, PostgreSQL. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: The system must provide end-to-end menu management with categories capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records; Menu Management with Categories should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate; Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to menu management with categories without leaving the module context.
acceptance_criteria: Core menu management with categories business rules are implemented for Restaurant Management System
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for menu management with categories
API/service behavior for menu management with categories is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Menu Management with Categories Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for menu management with categories with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The menu management with categories user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for menu management with categories behave correctly
The menu management with categories UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Harden Menu Management with Categories Security and Access Rules

FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for menu management with categories.
